sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Olympique de Marseille have shown mixed results in their last 5 matches, with impressive wins like 5-2 against Angers and 9-0 against Bayeux, but also a loss to Nantes (0-2). Liverpool, on the other hand, remain unbeaten in their last 12 games, though they've drawn three of their last five, including 1-1 against Burnley and 2-2 against Fulham, indicating some inconsistency in closing out matches.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In their last two encounters in 2008, Liverpool dominated with a 1-0 home win and a 2-1 away victory at Marseille's Orange Velodrome. Historically, Liverpool have won 3 of the last 5 matchups against Marseille, suggesting a psychological edge for the visitors.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Marseille, sitting 17th in the standings with 9 points, have scored 11 goals but struggle with creating big chances (ranked 27th). Liverpool, positioned 11th with 12 points, also have 11 goals but show better attacking intent with higher final third entries per game, though their recent finishing has been less clinical.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch
sections.text: Mason Greenwood has been in scintillating form for Marseille, netting 6 goals in his last 5 matches with a Sofascore rating of 7.84. For Liverpool, Florian Wirtz stands out with 3 goals and a rating of 7.6, potentially posing a threat with his creativity.

sections.subtitle: Match Outlook
sections.text: Given Sofascore's prediction (39% home win, 24% draw, 37% away win), this match appears closely contested. Marseille's home advantage and recent high-scoring form could challenge Liverpool, but the Reds’ unbeaten streak and historical edge might tilt the balance slightly in their favor.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: At half-time, Liverpool lead Olympique de Marseille by a score of 0-1 at the Orange Velodrome. The solitary goal came from Dominik Szoboszlai right at the end of the first half, capitalizing on a well-worked move. Marseille, despite having more ball possession, struggled to create significant chances, while Liverpool showed more incisiveness in the final third. The home side will need to find a way to penetrate Liverpool’s defense, while the away team looks poised to exploit counter-attacking opportunities.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Team statistics comparison
halftimeSections.text: Liverpool have dominated the key attacking metrics in the first half, with more final third entries and penalty area penetrations compared to Marseille. They also created a notable chance and converted it into a goal, reflecting their clinical approach. Marseille, on the other hand, held a slight edge in possession but failed to translate it into meaningful opportunities. Defensively, Liverpool were stronger in aerial duels and overall duels, which helped them maintain control during crucial moments. Marseille’s lack of big chances created highlights their struggle to break through Liverpool’s backline.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: The first half saw Liverpool starting with early attacking intent, pushing forward with several spells of pressure in the opening minutes. Marseille responded with brief periods of momentum, particularly around the middle of the half, but their attacks often fizzled out without serious threat. Liverpool regained dominance with consistent waves of pressure in the latter stages, culminating in the goal just before the break. The momentum shifted frequently, but Liverpool’s attacking spells were more sustained and dangerous compared to Marseille’s intermittent efforts.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key players to watch
halftimeSections.text: For Liverpool, Dominik Szoboszlai was the standout performer in the first half, not only scoring the opener but also showing composure in midfield. Mohamed Salah, despite not finding the net, remains a constant threat with his ability to create chances. On Marseille’s side, Amine Gouiri showed glimpses of danger with a shot on target, while Mason Greenwood’s involvement in the final third could be key if they are to mount a comeback. Defensive solidity will be crucial for both teams, with Virgil van Dijk anchoring Liverpool’s backline effectively so far.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Tactical outlook for second half
halftimeSections.text: Marseille are likely to come out with more urgency in the second half, pushing for an equalizer with increased attacking intent. They may look to exploit the flanks and deliver more crosses into the box to challenge Liverpool’s defense. Liverpool, meanwhile, could adopt a more measured approach, focusing on protecting their lead while seeking opportunities on the counter. Tactical adjustments from Marseille’s bench, possibly involving substitutions to inject fresh legs in attack, will be critical. Liverpool might prioritize midfield control to disrupt Marseille’s rhythm.
